content.problemStatement:
Chef bought a huge (effectively infinite) planar island and built NNN restaurants (numbered 111 through NNN) on it. For each valid iii, the Cartesian coordinates of restaurant iii are (Xi,Yi)(X_i, Y_i)(Xi‚Äč,Yi‚Äč).

Now, Chef wants to build N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 straight narrow roads (line segments) on the island. The roads may have arbitrary lengths; restaurants do not have to lie on the roads. The slope of each road must be 111 or ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1, i.e. for any two points (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1‚Äč,y1‚Äč) and (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2‚Äč,y2‚Äč) on the same road, ‚ą£x1‚ąíx2‚ą£=‚ą£y1‚ąíy2‚ą£|x_1-x_2| = |y_1-y_2|‚ą£x1‚Äč‚ąíx2‚Äč‚ą£=‚ą£y1‚Äč‚ąíy2‚Äč‚ą£ must hold.

Let's denote the minimum distance Chef has to walk from restaurant iii to reach a road by DiD_iDi‚Äč. Then, let's denote a=max‚ÄČ(D1,D2,‚Ä¶,DN)a = \mathrm{max}\,(D_1, D_2, \ldots, D_N)a=max(D1‚Äč,D2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,DN‚Äč); Chef wants this distance to be minimum possible.

Chef is a busy person, so he decided to give you the job of building the roads. You should find a way to build them that minimises aaa and compute a‚čÖ2a \cdot \sqrt{2}a‚čÖ2‚Äč.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers XiX_iXi‚Äč and YiY_iYi‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one real number ‚ÄĒ the minimum distance aaa multiplied by 2\sqrt{2}2‚Äč. Your answer will be considered correct if its absolute or relative error does not exceed 10‚ąí610^{-6}10‚ąí6.

Explanation:
Example case 1: We should build roads described by equations y‚ąíx+0.5=0y-x+0.5 = 0y‚ąíx+0.5=0 and y‚ąíx‚ąí0.5=0y-x-0.5 = 0y‚ąíx‚ąí0.5=0.
Example case 2: We should build roads described by equations y‚ąíx‚ąí1=0y-x-1 = 0y‚ąíx‚ąí1=0 and y+x‚ąí1=0y+x-1 = 0y+x‚ąí1=0.
